title: Add error when writing parameter with `$value$plusargs`
body:
```sv
module m #(
    string S = ""
);
  initial begin
    $value$plusargs("S=%s", S);
    #1;  // No error without this line
  end
endmodule
```

```
verilator --binary -MAKEFLAGS '--silent' --quiet -j 0 m.sv
```
v5.024-62-g d4b358330 on Archlinux

Output:
```
In file included from Vm__ALL.cpp:5:
Vm___024root__DepSet_hbd80a7bb__0.cpp: In function ‘VlCoroutine Vm___024root___eval_initial__TOP__Vtiming__0(Vm___024root*)’:
Vm___024root__DepSet_hbd80a7bb__0.cpp:23:31: error: binding reference of type ‘std::string&’ {aka ‘std::__cxx11::basic_string<char>&’} to ‘const std::string’ {aka ‘const std::__cxx11::basic_string<char>’} discards qualifiers
   23 |     (void)VL_VALUEPLUSARGS_INN(64, std::string{"S=%s"},
      |           ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
   24 |                                vlSelf->m__DOT__S);
      |                                ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
In file included from /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated.h:989,
                 from Vm__pch.h:21,
                 from Vm.cpp:4,
                 from Vm__ALL.cpp:3:
/usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_funcs.h:2478:76: note:   initializing argument 3 of ‘IData VL_VALUEPLUSARGS_INN(int, const std::string&, std::string&)’
 2478 | extern IData VL_VALUEPLUSARGS_INN(int, const std::string& ld, std::string& rdr) VL_MT_SAFE;
      |                                                               ~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~
```

Presumably the problem arises from writing to a parameter. Writing into a parameter may seem strange, however some tools accept this:

1. Vivado : yes
2. VCS: yes
3. Questa: no (sees a parameter as a non-modifiable variable)
4. Xcelium: no (refers to LRM 3.10, however I could not find any relevant sentence in LRM 2023. Maybe it refers to an older LRM).

IMHO: A parameter is like a port, so it should be set *at*(default value) or *outside* a module. So I find it ok to forbid this.
